system.name: Monster of the Week
system.description: A standalone action-horror RPG for 3-5 people. Hunters track down and destroy the monsters that most people don't believe in. Powered by the Apocalypse engine.
system.credits: Author: Michael Sands. Spell-Slinger playbook & layout: Fred Hicks. Editor: Steve Hickey. A hack of Apocalypse World by Vincent Baker.

playerCount.note: Works with more or fewer, but spotlight time per person changes accordingly.

system.sessionLength: About 2-4 hours per mystery; first session runs longer due to hunter creation.
system.versionNotes: v2.0.0: Surfaced bystander type->motivation in npcCreator; clarified minion-group harm and bystander armament build notes; added mysteryLetters and oneShot blocks.

ratings._comment: MotW calls its stats 'ratings'. Range -1 to +3. Added to 2d6 rolls. -1 bad, 0 average, +1 good, +2 really good, +3 phenomenal.

list.name: Charm
list.description: How pleasant and persuasive you are.

list.name: Cool
list.description: How calm and collected you are.

list.name: Tough
list.description: How strong and mean you are in a fight.

list.name: Weird
list.description: How attuned to the supernatural you are.

mechanic.default: Roll 2d6 and add the relevant rating (or another value a move specifies).

results.10+: Full success. You do what you set out to do.
results.7-9: Partial success. You succeed with a cost, hard choice, or worse outcome.
results.6-: Miss. The Keeper makes a move against you. Also mark experience.
results.12+: Only matters for advanced (basic) moves; grants an extraordinary result.

bonuses.forward: +1 (or stated amount) to your very next roll only.
bonuses.ongoing: +1 (or stated amount) to every relevant roll until the situation no longer applies.
bonuses.hold: A pool of points spent one-for-one for listed effects.

luck._comment: Each hunter starts with 7 Luck boxes (the Divine has a different track layout but same count). One-shot hunters start with 1-3.
spendOptions: After you roll, retroactively change the result to a 12.
Decrease a wound you just suffered to 0-harm, no matter how bad it was.
luck.whenSpent: Some playbooks trigger fate effects when Luck is spent (Chosen, Crooked, Spooky).
luck.whenEmpty: The hunter is 'doomed'. The Keeper may make hard moves against them at any time, and any fate/dark-side consequences begin to land.

harm._comment: Hunters track harm on a 7-box track; the fourth box marks the 'Unstable' threshold.

severity.description: Moderate. Improves with rest or first aid (heals 1).
Serious and unstable. Worsens until treated.
Kills a normal human, including a hunter.

harmMoves._comment: The Keeper picks a harm move appropriate to the injury's severity and nature.

healing.rest: Stable (1-3 harm) wounds heal 1 per day of rest.
healing.firstAid: Heals 1 harm per injury/condition; serious wounds stay unstable until properly treated.
healing.betweenMysteries: Full heal if there was time to rest; otherwise heal 1 harm.
healing.resurrection: Requires big magic, then roll -Weird (subtract your Weird) to see how strange you come back.

basicMoves.name: Act Under Pressure
basicMoves.trigger: You try to do something under stress or danger not covered by another move.

results.10+: You do what you set out to.
results.7-9: The Keeper gives you a worse outcome, hard choice, or price to pay.

basicMoves.advanced12+: Rise above the pressure: do what you wanted plus something extra, or do it to absolute perfection.

basicMoves.name: Help Out
basicMoves.trigger: You help another hunter with a move they are making. Help does not stack.

results.10+: Your help grants +1 to their roll.
results.7-9: Your help grants +1 to their roll, but you expose yourself to trouble or danger.
results.6-: You expose yourself to trouble or danger without helping.

basicMoves.advanced12+: You make the difference: the person you help acts as if they rolled a 12, regardless of their actual roll.

basicMoves.name: Investigate a Mystery
basicMoves.trigger: You investigate to learn about what's going on (interviews, tracking, research, forensics, etc.).

results.6-: You reveal information to the monster or whoever you're talking to; the Keeper may ask you questions.

holdQuestions: What sort of creature is it?
What was it going to do?
basicMoves.advanced12+: Ask the Keeper any question you want about the mystery, not just the listed ones.

basicMoves.name: Kick Some Ass
basicMoves.trigger: You get into a fight with something that's fighting back.

results.7+: You and your enemy inflict harm on each other (as established).
results.10+: As 7-9, plus choose one: gain the advantage (+1 forward to you or an ally); inflict terrible harm (+1 harm); suffer less harm (-1 harm); force them where you want them.
results.6-: You get your ass kicked: suffer harm or get captured, and inflict none back.

basicMoves.advanced12+: Devastate the enemy: inflict and suffer harm as normal, then pick one: all hunters get +1 forward; you suffer no harm; double your harm; drive the enemy away in a rout.

basicMoves.name: Manipulate Someone
basicMoves.trigger: You want someone to do something for you, and you give them a reason.

results.normal_10+: They'll do it for the reason given (or tell you the minimum it'd take).
results.normal_7-9: They'll do it, but only if you do something right now to show you mean it.
results.normal_6-: Your approach is wrong: you offend or anger them.
results.hunter_10+: If they do it, they mark experience and take +1 forward.
results.hunter_7-9: They mark experience if they do it.
results.hunter_6-: Up to them how badly you offend them; they mark experience if they refuse.

basicMoves.monsters: Doesn't work on monsters unless a move (e.g. Dark Negotiator) allows it; even then there's no guarantee they keep their word.
basicMoves.advanced12+: You absolutely convince them; a bystander may also become your ally.

basicMoves.name: Protect Someone
basicMoves.trigger: Someone is about to suffer harm and you try to prevent it.

results.7+: You protect them, but you'll suffer some or all of the harm they were going to get.
results.10+: Choose one extra: suffer little harm (-1); all impending danger focuses on you; inflict harm on the enemy; hold the enemy back.

basicMoves.advanced12+: Defend them perfectly: both you and the protected character are unharmed and out of danger; a protected bystander becomes your ally.

basicMoves.name: Read a Bad Situation
basicMoves.trigger: You look around and assess a hostile or dangerous situation.

results.6-: You misread it, or reveal tactical details to your enemies.

holdQuestions: Are there any dangers we haven't noticed?
What's the best way to protect the victims?
basicMoves.bonus: If you act on the answers, take +1 ongoing while the information is relevant.
basicMoves.advanced12+: Ask the Keeper any question you want about the situation, not just the listed ones.

basicMoves.name: Use Magic
basicMoves.trigger: You cast a spell or use an enchanted item. Say what you're trying to achieve and how.

results.10+: The magic works without issue: choose your effect.
results.7-9: It works imperfectly: choose your effect and a glitch (Keeper decides the glitch's impact).
results.6-: You lose control of the magic. This never ends well.

effects: Do one thing beyond human limitations.
Bar a place or portal to a specific person or type of creature.
Trap a specific person, minion, or monster.
Banish a spirit or curse from the person, object, or place it inhabits.
Summon a monster into the world.
Communicate with something you don't share a language with.
Heal 1-harm, cure a disease, or neutralize a poison.
glitches: The effect is of short duration.
The magic draws immediate, unwelcome attention.
It has a problematic side effect.
keeperRequirements: Takes 10 seconds, 30 seconds, or 1 minute to cast.
Needs one or two people to help cast.
Needs a tome of magic for the details.
basicMoves.advanced12+: You're infused with magical power: what you wanted happens, and the Keeper offers an extra benefit on top.

bigMagic._comment: For effects beyond the use-magic list (resurrection, dimensional portals, etc.). No roll for the magic itself; you must meet Keeper-set requirements.
requirements: Spend a lot of time (days/weeks) researching the ritual.
Experiment with the spell (many failures before success).
The casting takes a long time (hours/days).
Need a lot of people (2, 3, 7, 13, or more) to help.
Must be cast at a particular place and/or time.
Requires use-magic as part of the ritual.
Has a specific side effect or danger.

playbooks.name: The Chosen
playbooks.concept: A once-ordinary person with a prophesied destiny and the power to fulfill it. Good in a fight, with weird powers.

moves.name: Destiny's Plaything
moves.description: At the start of each mystery roll +Weird: 10+ the Keeper reveals a useful detail; 7-9 a vague hint; miss something bad happens to you.

moves.name: I'm Here For A Reason
moves.description: You're destined to do something; you can't die until it comes to pass (spend Luck if you would die). Once your task is done, all bets are off.

moves.name: The Big Entrance
moves.description: Make a showy entrance, roll +Cool to make enemies stop and listen.

moves.name: Devastating
moves.description: When you inflict harm, you may inflict +1 harm.

moves.name: Dutiful
moves.description: Act in accordance with a fate tag to mark experience; heroic tag also gives +1 forward.

moves.name: Invincible
moves.description: Always count as having 2-armour (doesn't stack).

moves.name: Resilience
moves.description: Heal an extra point whenever healed; wounds count as 1-harm less for Keeper harm moves.

fate.pick: Two heroic and two doom tags.

specialMechanics.specialWeapon: Choose a form, three business-end options, and a material.

playbooks.luckSpecial: When you spend Luck, the Keeper brings your fate into play.
playbooks.advancedImprovement: Delete one doom tag from your fate, and optionally change one heroic tag.

playbooks.name: The Crooked
playbooks.concept: A criminal turned monster hunter, haunted by their past and enemies. Good socially, with lots of contacts.

moves.name: Artifact
moves.description: You found and kept a magical artifact: protective amulet, lucky charm, grimoire, skeleton key, or imp stone.

moves.name: Crew
moves.description: You have a regular crew of three or four who help with anything (counts as a team).

moves.name: Deal with the Devil
moves.description: You sold your soul; pick one or two things you got. Payment is due eventually.

moves.name: Friends on the Force
moves.description: Act under pressure to contact cops who'll look the other way (for a cost).

moves.name: Made
moves.description: You're 'made' in a gang; you can call on members but owe loyalty.

moves.name: Driver
moves.description: +1 ongoing while driving; can hotwire anything; own two handy vehicles.

moves.name: Home Ground
moves.description: In your old neighbourhood you can always find people to hide or help you.

moves.name: Notorious
moves.description: Your criminal reputation counts as a reason for people to do what you ask when you reveal who you are.

specialMechanics.heat: Pick at least two enemies from your past and name them.
specialMechanics.underworld: Pick how you discovered the real underworld.

playbooks.luckSpecial: When you spend Luck, someone from your past (heat or underworld) reappears soon.

playbooks.name: The Divine
playbooks.concept: An agent of a higher power with a mission. Very tough, with holy powers.

moves.name: Boss from Beyond
moves.description: Start of mystery, roll +Weird to receive (and question) orders from your Superiors.

moves.name: Angel Wings
moves.description: Teleport to anywhere you've visited or a person you know; roll +Weird to carry others.

moves.name: What I Need, When I Need It
moves.description: Store small objects in a magical space and retrieve them at will.

moves.name: Soothe
moves.description: Calm someone with a few quiet words, blocking panic, anger, or negative emotions.

moves.name: Lay On Hands
moves.description: Roll +Cool to heal injury or disease by touch.

moves.name: Cast Out Evil
moves.description: Roll +Tough to banish an unnatural creature from your presence.

moves.name: Smite
moves.description: Your body and divine weapon count as a weakness vs monsters; unarmed attacks are 2-harm intimate hand messy.

mission: Fight the schemes of an Adversary
Ensure the End of Days comes to pass
Work for Good in exile, hidden from your kind
Prepare and protect a specific other hunter

gear: One divine weapon (flaming sword / thunder hammer / razor whip / five demon bag / silver trident, all holy)
playbooks.luckTrack: Same 7-box count, displayed differently on the sheet.

playbooks.name: The Expert
playbooks.concept: The hunter who knows all about monsters and magic, with a well-supplied base.
playbooks.startOfMysteryMove: optional (The Woman/Man With The Plan, Dark Past)

moves.name: I've Read About This Sort Of Thing
moves.description: Roll +Sharp instead of +Cool when you act under pressure.

moves.name: Often Right
moves.description: Give honest advice; if a hunter follows it they get +1 ongoing and you mark experience.

moves.name: Preparedness
moves.description: Roll +Sharp to have an unusual or rare item on hand.

moves.name: It Wasn't As Bad As It Looked
moves.description: Once per mystery, roll +Cool to keep going despite injuries.

moves.name: Precise Strike
moves.description: Roll +Tough to aim for a weak spot and inflict bonus harm.

moves.name: The Woman (or Man) With The Plan
moves.description: Start of mystery, roll +Sharp to hold and spend to be prepared and in the right place.

moves.name: Dark Past
moves.description: Roll +Weird to recall relevant black-magic knowledge (with a risk of complicity).

gear: Three monster-slaying weapons (stakes / silver sword / cold iron sword / blessed knife / magical dagger / juju bag / flamethrower / magnum / shotgun)

playbooks.name: The Flake
playbooks.concept: A conspiracy theorist, great at finding things out and connecting separate mysteries.
examples: Agent Mulder / The Lone Gunmen (The X-Files)

moves.name: Connect the Dots
moves.description: Start of mystery, roll +Sharp to hold and spend asking how events connect to the bigger picture.

moves.name: Crazy Eyes
moves.description: +1 Weird (max +3).

moves.name: See, It All Fits Together
moves.description: Use Sharp instead of Charm when you manipulate someone.

moves.name: Suspicious Mind
moves.description: If someone lies to you, you know it.

moves.name: Often Overlooked
moves.description: Roll +Weird to act crazy and be regarded as unthreatening/unimportant.

moves.name: Contrary
moves.description: Seek honest advice then do something else to mark experience (opposite = +1 ongoing).

moves.name: Net Friends
moves.description: Roll +Charm to get internet contacts to help with a mystery.

moves.name: Sneaky
moves.description: Attack from ambush or behind for +2 harm.

playbooks.name: The Initiate
playbooks.concept: A trained member of an ancient monster-slaying Sect. Good with magic; the Sect provides help and problems.

moves.name: Sect Standing
moves.description: When in good standing, start of mystery roll +Charm for Sect help; a miss means they ask something bad.

moves.name: Ancient Fighting Arts
moves.description: +1 harm and +1 to protect someone with an old-fashioned hand weapon.

moves.name: Mystic
moves.description: +1 forward every time you successfully use magic.

moves.name: Fortunes
moves.description: Once per mystery roll +Weird to hold and spend on prophecy/divination effects.

moves.name: Sacred Oath
moves.description: Bind yourself to a goal and forsake something; mark experience and +1 on goal-related rolls while you keep it.

moves.name: Mentor
moves.description: Roll +Sharp to get answers from your named Sect mentor.

moves.name: Apprentice
moves.description: You have a named apprentice who counts as a subordinate ally.

moves.name: Helping Hand
moves.description: Successful help out gives +2 instead of +1.

moves.name: That Old Black Magic
moves.description: Use magic to ask an investigate-a-mystery question as your effect.

sect.pick: Two good traditions and one bad tradition.

gear: Old-fashioned and/or modern weapons (count depends on Sect traditions)
playbooks.advancedImprovement: Become the leader, or effective leader, of the whole Sect.

playbooks.name: The Monstrous
playbooks.concept: A monster fighting for the good guys. Very weird, with powers based on its breed.

moves.name: Immortal
moves.description: You don't age or sicken, and suffer 1-harm less whenever harmed.

moves.name: Unnatural Appeal
moves.description: Roll +Weird instead of +Charm when you manipulate someone.

moves.name: Unholy Strength
moves.description: Roll +Weird instead of +Tough when you kick some ass.

moves.name: Incorporeal
moves.description: Move freely through solid objects (not people).

moves.name: Preternatural Speed
moves.description: +1 ongoing when you chase, flee, or run.

moves.name: Claws of the Beast
moves.description: All natural attacks get +1 harm.

moves.name: Mental Dominion
moves.description: Roll +Charm to hold and command normal humans.

moves.name: Unquenchable Vitality
moves.description: Roll +Cool to heal yourself.

moves.name: Dark Negotiator
moves.description: Use manipulate someone on monsters that reason and talk.

moves.name: Flight
moves.description: You can fly.

moves.name: Shapeshifter
moves.description: Change form; +1 to investigate a mystery using an alternate form's senses.

moves.name: Something Borrowed
moves.description: Take a move from a hunter playbook not currently in play.

extras: an extra range on a base

playbooks.advancedImprovement: Free yourself from the curse (lose 1 Weird); or turn evil again (retire as a Keeper threat).

playbooks.name: The Mundane
playbooks.concept: A normal person, great with regular people and at getting captured (more useful than it sounds).

moves.name: Always The Victim
moves.description: When protected, the protector marks experience; when captured by a monster, you mark experience.

moves.name: Oops!
moves.description: Stumble across something important and useful (not necessarily related to your problem).

moves.name: Let's Get Out Of Here!
moves.description: Protect someone by leading them out, rolling +Charm instead of +Tough.

moves.name: Panic Button
moves.description: Roll +Sharp to escape via a named route.

moves.name: The Power of Heart
moves.description: When you help someone fighting a monster, you automatically help as a 10+.

moves.name: Trust Me
moves.description: Roll +Charm to tell a normal person the truth to protect them.

moves.name: What Could Go Wrong?
moves.description: Charge into danger to hold 2 for combat effects.

moves.name: Don't Worry, I'll Check It Out
moves.description: Go off alone somewhere scary to mark experience.

playbooks.advancedImprovement: Erase a used Luck mark (also a standard improvement option).

playbooks.name: The Professional
playbooks.concept: You work for an agency that hunts monsters. Good in a fight and a strong team player.

moves.name: Deal with the Agency
moves.description: Roll +Sharp when requesting help/gear or making excuses to the Agency.

moves.name: Bottle It Up
moves.description: Take up to +3 on act under pressure; the Keeper holds 1 per +1 for later penalties.

moves.name: Unfazeable
moves.description: +1 Cool (max +3).

moves.name: Battlefield Awareness
moves.description: +1 armour (max 2) on top of gear.

moves.name: Leave No One Behind
moves.description: Roll +Sharp to help someone escape combat cleanly.

moves.name: Tactical Genius
moves.description: Roll +Cool instead of +Sharp when you read a bad situation.

moves.name: Medic
moves.description: Roll +Cool for first aid; replaces regular first aid.

moves.name: Mobility
moves.description: A monster-hunting vehicle with two good and one bad trait.

agency.pick: Two resource tags and two red tape tags.

gear: Flak vest (1-armour hidden) or combat armour (2-armour heavy)
playbooks.advancedImprovement: Get some or all of the other hunters hired by your agency.

playbooks.name: The Spell-Slinger
playbooks.concept: A trained wizard wielding powerful combat magic. Arcane training helps with eldritch secrets.

moves.name: Tools and Techniques
moves.description: Combat magic relies on Consumables, Foci, Gestures, and Incantations. Cross off one; the rest have drawbacks if unavailable.

moves.name: Advanced Arcane Training
moves.description: If you have two of three Tools and Techniques ready, ignore the third.

moves.name: Arcane Reputation
moves.description: Three supernatural groups respect you; +1 forward to manipulate affected humans.

moves.name: Could've Been Worse
moves.description: On a use-magic miss, choose Fizzle or This Is Gonna Suck instead of losing control.

moves.name: Enchanted Clothing
moves.description: An enchanted garment gives -1 harm from sources that reach you through it.

moves.name: Forensic Divination
moves.description: On a successful investigate, ask 'What magic was done here?' for free.

moves.name: Go Big or Go Home
moves.description: +1 ongoing to use-magic rolls required for Big Magic.

moves.name: Not My Fault
moves.description: +1 to act under pressure dealing with your own spellcasting consequences.

moves.name: Practitioner
moves.description: Choose two use-magic effects; +1 to use magic when choosing them.

moves.name: Shield Spell
moves.description: When you protect someone, gain 2-armour against transferred harm.

moves.name: Third Eye
moves.description: When you read a bad situation, +1 hold on 7+ and see invisible things (miss exposes you).

combatMagic.pick: Three options, at least one base.
bases: Ball (1-harm magical area close obvious loud)
Wall (1-harm magical barrier close 1-armour obvious loud)

gear: One backup weapon (old revolver / ritual knife / heirloom sword)
playbooks.advancedImprovement: Cross off another option from your Tools and Techniques.

playbooks.name: The Spooky
playbooks.concept: Has strange, sinister psychic or magical powers not fully under their control.

moves.name: Telepathy
moves.description: Read thoughts and project words; investigate, read situations, and manipulate without speaking.

moves.name: Hex
moves.description: When you use magic, add a disease, immediate harm, or breakage to the target.

moves.name: The Sight
moves.description: See and communicate with the invisible; more clues when you investigate.

moves.name: Premonitions
moves.description: Start of mystery roll +Weird for a vision of something bad to come.

moves.name: Hunches
moves.description: Roll +Sharp to know where to be when something bad happens elsewhere.

moves.name: Tune In
moves.description: Roll +Weird to attune to a monster/minion and hold for questions.

moves.name: The Big Whammy
moves.description: Kick some ass with +Weird; 2-harm close obvious ignore-armour (miss = backlash).

moves.name: Jinx
moves.description: Roll +Weird to encourage coincidences and hold for effects.

gear: Magical items/amulets used to invoke powers
playbooks.luckSpecial: As you spend Luck, your dark side's demands grow nastier.
playbooks.advancedImprovement: Delete one dark side tag permanently.

playbooks.name: The Wronged
playbooks.concept: Revenge-driven and very tough. Focused on killing a specific breed of monster and protecting others.

moves.name: I Know My Prey
moves.description: +1 ongoing when knowingly investigating, pursuing, or fighting the breed that caused your loss.

moves.name: Berserk
moves.description: Keep going until the fight ends; the Keeper can't use harm moves and you can't die mid-fight (harm lands afterward).

moves.name: NEVER AGAIN
moves.description: Protect someone without rolling as a 10+, but can't choose 'suffer little harm'.

moves.name: What Does Not Kill Me...
moves.description: +1 ongoing until the fight ends once you've suffered harm in it.

moves.name: Fervor
moves.description: Roll +Tough instead of +Charm to manipulate someone.

moves.name: Safety First
moves.description: +1 armour (max 2) from jury-rigged gear.

moves.name: DIY Surgery
moves.description: Roll +Cool for quick and dirty first aid on yourself or others.

moves.name: Tools Matter
moves.description: +1 to kick some ass with your signature weapon.

loss.prey: Pick the monster breed (with the Keeper).

playbooks.advancedImprovement: Track down the specific monster(s) responsible (next mystery is about them); or change the target of I Know My Prey.

extraPlaybooks._comment: Official and fan playbooks beyond the core twelve; mostly available on the MotW website.

teamConcepts._comment: Optional shared reasons the hunters work together, picked during the first session.

examples.name: Slayer and Entourage
examples.description: A Chosen at the centre, supported by mentors and friends.

examples.name: The Unexplained Cases Team
examples.description: An agency team handling the weird stuff; requires a Professional.

examples.name: The Order
examples.description: Members of an ancient order of monster slayers; requires an Initiate.

examples.name: Road-tripping Hunter Family
examples.description: A nomadic family trade of hunting monsters.

standard: Get +1 to a rating (up to the playbook max).
Take another move from your playbook.
Take a move from another playbook.
Gain an ally or a team.
Gain a haven (Expert-style) / add a haven option.
Playbook-specific options (e.g. Crooked stash, Initiate chapter command).
advanced: Change your hunter to a new type.
Create a second hunter to play as well.
Mark two basic moves as advanced (unlocks 12+ results).
Erase / get back one used Luck point.
improvements.leveling: Mark experience on a miss (6-) or when a move says to. Five marks = level up; pick an improvement and reset. After five level-ups, advanced improvements unlock.
endOfSessionQuestions: Did we conclude the current mystery?
Did we save someone from certain death (or worse)?
Did we learn something new and important about the world?
Did we learn something new and important about one of the hunters?
improvements.endOfSessionExperience: 1-2 yes answers = mark 1 experience each; 3-4 = mark 2.
improvements.legacyExperienceRules: First-edition option: don't mark XP on a miss; instead highlight two ratings per session (one chosen by another hunter, one by the Keeper) and mark XP whenever a highlighted rating is rolled.

allyTypes.motivation: to execute the spirit of your instructions
to give you advice and perspective

weaponTags._comment: Every weapon needs a harm tag and a range tag; others are optional descriptors.
harmGuidelines: 0-harm: hardly more dangerous than a punch.

descriptors.area: Can hit multiple foes; divide harm among targets.
descriptors.balanced: Easy to wield and keep grip on.
descriptors.barrier: Harms anything passing through; counts as armour against attacks through it.
descriptors.forceful: Pushes things around; lets you force them where you want.
descriptors.holy: More effective against monsters weak to holy items.
descriptors.ignore-armour: Armour has no effect (magic armour needs magic + ignore-armour to bypass).
descriptors.life-drain: Heals the wielder for harm inflicted.
descriptors.magic: Enchanted; affects creatures/armour proof against normal weapons.
descriptors.many: Small enough to carry a large number.
descriptors.quick: Fast to prepare/attack; better chance to go first.
descriptors.restraining: A successful attack entangles or grabs the victim.
descriptors.unreliable: Needs maintenance or it may fail.
descriptors.valuable: Antique or made of valuable materials.

armour.1: Heavy leather jacket, motorcycle armour, bullet/stab-proof vest.
armour.2: Military body armour, riot gear, medieval mail.
armour.note: Armour reduces harm suffered by its rating. Magic armour ignores normal ignore-armour attacks.

agenda: Make the hunters' lives dangerous and scary.
principles: Address yourself to the hunters, not the players.
Use the Keeper moves, never use their names.
Be a fan of the hunters.
Build up a coherent mythology of the world as you play.
Nothing is safe. Kill bystanders and minions, burn down buildings, let monsters be slain.
Name everyone they meet, make them seem like normal folks.
Ask questions and build on the answers.
Sometimes give the hunters exactly what they earned, rather than everything they wanted.
You don't always have to decide what happens.
alwaysSay: What your prepared mystery and arcs demand.
basicKeeperMoves: Tell them the possible consequences and ask if they want to go ahead.
Turn their move back on them.
Offer an opportunity, maybe with a cost.
Take away some of the hunters' stuff.
Make a threat move (from a mystery or arc threat).
After every move, ask what they do next.

moveHardness.soft: Threatens danger or pushes the hunters, leaving room to react.
moveHardness.hard: No chance to react; used on a golden opportunity, a missed roll (6-), or against a doomed hunter.

threats._comment: Threats make trouble for the hunters. Each has a type and a corresponding motivation, and may have custom moves.

types.motivation: to run wild, destroying and killing
to give birth to, bring forth, or create evil
to steal specific sorts of things
to bring about the end of the world
to tempt people into evil deeds

weaknessTypes: Something that harms it directly (e.g. silver).
An environment that weakens/harms it (e.g. sunlight).
A required ritual or action (e.g. an exorcism).
moves: Attack with great force and fury
Order underlings to do terrible acts
Escape, no matter how well contained it is
Boast and gloat, maybe revealing a secret
monster.killNote: A monster only truly dies once its weakness is used against it, no matter how much harm it has taken.

types.motivation: to save their own skin at any cost
to bar a way or protect something
to push victims towards the monster
to steal and deliver to the monster

minion.buildNote: Like monsters but usually weaker. Attacks 1-4 harm; harm capacity 5-10; armour rare. A group of nameless minions can share one harm track with a +1 attack boost: the track represents defeating the group as a whole, and each point of harm inflicted removes a chunk of the group (e.g. Damn Dirty Apes: 1 harm = ~3 monkeys out of action).
moves: A burst of sudden, uncontrolled violence
Deliver someone or something to the master
Make a threat or demand on behalf of the master
Display a hint of conscience or humanity
Disobey the master, in some petty way

types.motivation: to interfere in other people's plans

bystander.buildNote: Need a name, description, type & motivation. Include at least two so the hunters have people to save. Default unknown people to witness (has clues) or victim (otherwise). Most bystanders default to 7-harm; only stat a weapon if they're likely to fight (helpers especially).

types.motivation: to bring people, and things, together

location.buildNote: Pick the type by what the place is emotionally/functionally, not its literal form. Optionally add a map and custom moves.

mysteryCreation.countdownNote: Six escalating events that happen if the hunters never interfere. Step names are metaphorical (timing/severity, not literal time of day). Used as a flexible guide once the hunters get involved.

scope.personal: Each countdown event stays focused on one person or their immediate surroundings.
scope.epic: Each event widens in scale, potentially up to a world-ending threat.

templates.name: A Bug Hunt
templates.summary: A straightforward monster hunt. Suits beasts, devourers, torturers, executioners.

templates.name: An Evil Ritual
templates.summary: A monster or sorcerer plans a terrible ritual to unleash horror.

templates.name: Not What It Seems
templates.summary: Starts looking like one monster, turns out to be something else.

messingWithTheFormula: No monster at all (weird phenomenon or natural cause)

mysteryLetters._comment: A tool to hand a hunter info that sets up a mystery and starts in the action. Especially useful for writing a player back in after they miss a session, after a hiatus, for convention/one-shot games, or to open with a roar.
whenToUse: A player missed the last session (bring them and you back up to speed).
Running a convention or one-shot game.
There's been a hiatus in your game.
There's unfinished business from the last session.
You want to give the hunters clues going in.
You want to start with the hunters already in the midst of the action.
structure: Briefly remind the hunter what happened last time and where you left off.
Include any new info, background, or the hook for this mystery.
End with a custom move to roll (or a list of options to pick from) that hands over useful info, drops them into action, or both.
moveTips: An information-finding move can offer questions (10+ pick two, 7-9 pick one, miss ask one but it cost you something).
An into-the-action move can offer outcomes to pick (10+ pick two, 7-9 pick one, miss the Keeper narrates what's going wrong).
Phrase one option negatively ('You do NOT have a shard of glass in your leg') so the hunter must pick it to avoid trouble.
Use options to hint at things beyond the immediate scene (a map that matters, other threats not yet mentioned).

oneShot._comment: Running a single self-contained mystery (convention game or a change of pace). Maps onto a live test/one-off show.
hunters: Start with fewer Luck: 1-3 depending on desired lethality.
Optionally grant a free level-up after creation to offset the reduced Luck.
Pick a team concept and tell everyone who the team is and how they operate.
You may make certain playbooks mandatory, suggest fitting ones, and leave others out entirely.
Still let each player build their own hunter (even from a restricted set) for buy-in.
You may reduce creation options to suit the mystery (e.g. flag which moves are useful, disallowed, or irrelevant).
mysteryCreation: A twist or two works very well (e.g. a hook that looks like a classic monster but is something else).
Bring a map, a list of names, and a few pre-written bystanders (a suspicious cop and some naive victims always fit).
Feel free to ramp danger: more powers, more minions/monsters, higher-harm attacks, higher harm capacity, threats targeting hunters or loved ones directly.
Scale can be huge (epidemics, Hell unleashed, gods from beyond) since there's no continuing game to protect.
Tie in any central story elements (Chosen's fate, Wronged's nemesis, Professional's Agency, Initiate's Sect) of the playbooks at the table.
play: Follow first-session structure; give a quick rules intro (moves + ratings) before creation, explain the rest as needed.
Go easier on world-building questions — you don't need to bank as many answers.
Go harder in play: push for more horror, don't hold back on danger.

arcCreation._comment: Arcs are long-term, slow-burning plots running in the background (season-long threats).
arcCreation.countdownNote: Like a mystery countdown but each step could be a whole mystery. For player-triggered arcs, it's what happens if the hunters don't stop the plan.

templates.name: Ancient Evil Rising
templates.summary: A dormant/imprisoned monster and the servants working to free it.

templates.name: The Nemesis
templates.summary: A monster (often an escapee or the Wronged's target) hunts the hunters.

templates.name: The End of Days
templates.summary: The apocalypse; the world is going to end.

templates.name: Corruption
templates.summary: A trusted organisation has been infiltrated by, or always was, evil.

customMoves._comment: Build custom moves for unique threat powers, special situations, or new hunter abilities.
structure: Define when it triggers (a specific condition).
types: A roll (usually adding a rating), with 10+/7-9/6- outcomes.
A bonus or penalty for some circumstance.
A special effect in certain circumstances.

npcCreator._comment: Simplified flow for audience-built bystanders at live shows. Maps directly onto the bystander threat structure (name, description, type/motivation). Internal app model uses 'NPC'; audience-facing copy uses 'character'.

fields.label: Character Name
fields.helpText: Evocative names land better — 'Mrs. Henderson' tells a different story than 'Kayla.'

fields.label: Who are they in town?
fields.helpText: How this person fits into the world the hunters are investigating.

fields.label: Appearance
fields.placeholder: Describe them in a sentence or two.

fields.label: What do they know (or suspect)?
fields.placeholder: A rumor, a thing they saw, a secret...
fields.helpText: Every bystander is sitting on something — a clue, a lie, or a fear.

fields.label: What kind of person are they?
fields.helpText: Sets their motivation — how they'll make trouble for (or help) the hunters. The motivation IS the type; show it when this is selected.

npcCreator._motivationDisplayNote: Render each option as 'Type — motivation' (e.g. 'Skeptic — to deny supernatural explanations') so the host knows what the character will do. Source of truth is threats.bystander.types.

typeMotivations.busybody: to interfere in other people's plans

showConfig._comment: Per-show overrides for a Monster of the Week live show at Lucky Straws. Populate when a MotW show is scheduled. Left as a template scaffold for now.
